metadata.source: Historical archives, Royal Thai Government Gazettes, corporate histories, and economic development reports of Thailand
metadata.description: Comprehensive dataset of Thai actors driving technical change. Batch 1 of 5.
metadata.introduction: A granular view of the dynamics structuring technical change in Thailand. This batch covers foundational state apparatus, key research institutes, major universities, and strategic industrial conglomerates. Events are detailed to show strategic evolution from the Rattanakosin modernization era to Industry 4.0.

actors.name: Ministry of Higher Education, Science, Research and Innovation (MHESI)
actors.description: Principal government body integrating higher education, science, research, and innovation policy.

events.description: Predecessor Ministry of Science, Technology and Energy established.
Renamed Ministry of Science, Technology and Environment (MOSTE).
Reorganized into Ministry of Science and Technology (MOST).
Royal Decree established MHESI, merging science and higher education mandates.

actors.name: National Science and Technology Development Agency (NSTDA)
actors.description: Umbrella organization for national research centers (BIOTEC, MTEC, NECTEC, etc.).

events.description: Established initially as the Science and Technology Development Board.
Formally inaugurated as NSTDA under MOSTE.
Relocated to Thailand Science Park, creating a dedicated R&D cluster.
Launched TSP Phase 2 to expand laboratory and incubation space.

actors.name: Chulalongkorn University
actors.description: Thailand's oldest and most prestigious university, central to technical elite production.

events.description: Founded as the Royal Pages School (Civil Service Training).
Formally established as Chulalongkorn University, named after King Rama V.
Established the Faculty of Engineering, formalizing technical education.
Established the Institute of Science and Technology for Research.

actors.name: Siam Cement Group (SCG)
actors.description: Thailand's largest industrial conglomerate, driving construction and chemical technology.

events.description: Established by Royal Decree of King Rama VI to produce domestic cement.
Diversified into petrochemicals, founding Thai Plastic Industrial.
Established the SCG Training Center to elevate technical skills in manufacturing.
Launched 'SCG Innovation Hub' to scout and invest in deep-tech startups.

actors.name: PTT Public Company Limited
actors.description: State-owned energy giant driving downstream industrialization and refining.

events.description: Established as Petroleum Authority of Thailand.
Commissioned the first Natural Gas Separation Plant in Rayong.
Successfully listed on the Stock Exchange of Thailand (SET).
Announced 'PTT Synergy' strategy, pivoting to electric mobility infrastructure.

actors.name: Board of Investment (BOI)
actors.description: Key agency for attracting FDI and shaping industrial technology policy through incentives.

events.description: Passed new Investment Promotion Act, shifting focus to export-oriented industries.
Introduced specific incentives for 'High Technology' projects and R&D.
Launched 'Cluster Development' policy targeting 10 strategic industries including robotics and aviation.

actors.name: King Mongkut's University of Technology Thonburi (KMUTT)
actors.description: Leading technical university focusing on engineering and industrial technology.

events.description: Upgraded to 'King Mongkut's Institute of Technology Thonburi'.
Established the 'Theoretical and Applied Science Research Center'.

actors.name: Electricity Generating Authority of Thailand (EGAT)
actors.description: State-owned utility responsible for power generation and transmission grid.

events.description: Established under the 'Yankee' power development plan.
Commissioned Bhumibol Dam, a major hydroelectric power source.
Piloted the 'Pang Khon' wind power plant, entering renewable energy era.

actors.name: Advanced Info Service (AIS)
actors.description: Thailand's largest mobile network operator, driving digital adoption.

events.description: Founded as IT services provider under Shinawatra Computer.
Launched commercial 5G service at SEA Games.

actors.name: National Economic and Social Development Council (NESDC)
actors.description: Architect of the National Economic and Social Development Plans.

events.description: Established as the National Economic Council.
Launched the First National Economic Development Plan.
Renamed to NESDC to emphasize social aspects.

actors.name: Charoen Pokphand Group (CP Group)
actors.description: Leading conglomerate integrating agro-industrial technology and telecommunications (True).

events.description: Founded as a small seed shop in Bangkok's Chinatown.
Introduced contract farming, revolutionizing agricultural supply chains.
Entered telecom sector, founding TelecomAsia (now True).

actors.name: Thailand Institute of Scientific and Technological Research (TISTR)
actors.description: Applied research institute focusing on industrial standards and testing.

events.description: Established as the Applied Scientific Research Corporation of Thailand.
Transformed into a state enterprise under MOSTE.
Achieved ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation for testing labs.

actors.name: Asian Institute of Technology (AIT)
actors.description: International institute for engineering and technology education.

events.description: Founded as SEATO Graduate School of Engineering.
Launched 'Geoinformatics Center' for regional spatial technology.

actors.name: Software Park Thailand
actors.description: Government agency promoting software industry development.

events.description: Opened the main building in Chaeng Watthana.

actors.name: Mahidol University
actors.description: Premier institution for medical and life sciences research.

events.description: Renamed Mahidol University in honor of Prince Mahidol.
Opened the 'Mahidol Medical Genomics Center' for precision medicine.

actors.name: Thai Airways International
actors.description: National flag carrier, driving tourism and aviation maintenance standards.

events.description: Founded as a joint venture between domestic carrier and SAS.
Received first Douglas DC-10 aircraft, modernizing fleet.
Listed on the Stock Exchange of Thailand.

actors.name: National Broadcasting and Telecommunications Commission (NBTC)
actors.description: Regulatory body for broadcasting and telecommunications spectrum.

events.description: Established under the Frequency Allocation Act.
Conducted first 3G spectrum auction, ending state monopoly.

actors.name: Siam Commercial Bank (SCB)
actors.description: Thailand's first bank, driving digital banking innovation.

events.description: Established as the 'Book Club' before formal banking license.
Formally registered as Siam Commercial Bank.
Launched SCB Easy version 8.0, a leading digital banking platform.

actors.name: Bangkok Bank
actors.description: Thailand's largest bank by assets, financing industrial growth.

events.description: Opened first overseas branch in Hong Kong.
Implemented core banking system upgrade for regional integration.

actors.name: Thai Summit Group
actors.description: Leading manufacturer of automotive parts and assemblies.

events.description: Joint venture with Honda for motorcycle frame manufacturing.
Adopted robotic welding lines for chassis production.

actors.name: Synchrotron Light Research Institute (SLRI)
actors.description: Operator of Thailand's synchrotron light source facility.

events.description: First light achieved at the Siam Photon Source.
Initiated project to upgrade storage ring for higher brightness.

actors.name: National Institute of Metrology (Thailand) (NIMT)
actors.description: National measurement institute ensuring industrial standardization.

events.description: Became a member of the Meter Convention (CIPM MRA).

actors.name: Industrial Estate Authority of Thailand (IEAT)
actors.description: State enterprise responsible for developing and managing industrial estates.

events.description: Established to organize industrial land use.
Launched Bangchan Industrial Estate, the first in Thailand.
Introduced IEAT Free Zone incentives for export industries.

actors.name: Amata Corporation
actors.description: Largest private developer of industrial estates.

events.description: Developed Amata Nakorn Industrial Estate in Chonburi.
Initiated 'Amata Smart City' concept for industrial digitization.

actors.name: King Mongkut's University of Technology North Bangkok (KMUTNB)
actors.description: Technical university originating from Thai-German technical cooperation.

events.description: Upgraded to North Bangkok Technical College.

actors.name: Thai Oil Public Company Limited
actors.description: Thailand's largest oil refinery, critical for energy security.

events.description: Founded as Thai Oil Refinery Co., Ltd.
Completed Clean Fuel Project to produce Euro IV standard fuels.
Founded to represent the manufacturing sector.

actors.name: Thailand Automotive Institute (TAI)
actors.description: Policy think tank for the automotive industry.

actors.name: Delta Electronics (Thailand)
actors.description: Leading manufacturer of power electronics and automation components.

events.description: Listed on the Stock Exchange of Thailand.

actors.name: King Mongkut's Institute of Technology Ladkrabang (KMITL)
actors.description: Premier engineering institute, strong in telecommunications and electronics.

events.description: Established under the Ministry of Digital Economy and Society.

actors.name: Hana Microelectronics
actors.description: Leading semiconductor assembly and test service provider.

actors.name: Office of the National Economic and Social Development Board (NESDB)
actors.description: Predecessor to NESDC, key in drafting 5-year plans.

events.description: Established by Prime Minister Sarit Thanarat.
Integrated social development into economic planning.

actors.name: Bangkok Expressway and Metro (BEM)
actors.description: Major operator of expressways and metro systems.

events.description: Founded as Expressway and Rapid Transit Authority (ETA).
Restructured as BEM (Bangkok Expressway and Metro).
Opened Blue Line MRT, Thailand's first underground rail.

actors.name: Bank of Thailand (BOT)
actors.description: Central bank overseeing financial stability and FinTech innovation.

events.description: Founded under the Bank of Thailand Act.
Launched retail Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) pilot.

actors.name: Thai Union Group
actors.description: Global leader in seafood processing and innovation.

events.description: Founded as Thai Union Manufacturing Co., Ltd.
Opened Global Innovation Incubator (GII) in Bangkok.

actors.name: True Corporation
actors.description: Integrated telecom and media provider (CP Group).

events.description: Launched broadband internet services over ADSL.
Merged with True Move to form True Corporation.

actors.name: Suranaree University of Technology (SUT)
actors.description: First public autonomous university, focused on science and technology.

events.description: Established as the first autonomous university outside Bangkok.
Hosts the 'Institute of Engineering' emphasizing cooperative education.

actors.name: Geo-Informatics and Space Technology Development Agency (GISTDA)
actors.description: National space agency for remote sensing and GIS.

events.description: Established under Ministry of Science and Technology.
Launched THEOS-1 (Thaichote), Thailand's first earth observation satellite.
Initiated development of THEOS-2 satellite system.

actors.name: Chiang Mai University (CMU)
actors.description: First provincial university, hub for Northern Thailand's technology.

events.description: Founded under the National Education Plan.
Opened 'Center of Excellence in Materials Science and Nanotechnology'.

actors.name: WHA Corporation
actors.description: Leading developer of industrial estates and logistics hubs.

events.description: Founded as Warehouse & Habit Co., Ltd.

actors.name: National Metal and Materials Technology Center (MTEC)
actors.description: Research center under NSTDA for advanced materials.

events.description: Developed lightweight magnesium alloy for automotive industry.

actors.name: National Center for Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology (BIOTEC)
actors.description: Pioneering biotech research center under NSTDA.

events.description: Developed diagnostic kits for shrimp viruses (white spot syndrome).

actors.name: National Electronics and Computer Technology Center (NECTEC)
actors.description: Core driver of Thailand's IT and computer technology policy.

events.description: Launched 'ThaiSarn' (Thai Social/Scientific Academic Network), early internet.
Developed 'Chula Nakhon' rice sensor for moisture detection.

actors.name: Kasetsart University
actors.description: Premier university for agriculture and applied sciences.

actors.name: Irámu Technology Company (Iramutech)
actors.description: Leading company in mining and metallurgical technology.

events.description: Adopted flotation technology for potassium separation.

actors.name: Office of the Civil Service Commission (OCSC)
actors.description: Regulates civil service, key in technical manpower planning.

events.description: Established by the Civil Service Act.
Initiated overseas scholarship programs for technical disciplines.

actors.name: Ministry of Industry
actors.description: Regulates factories, industrial standards, and SMEs.

events.description: Promulgated the Factory Act, modernizing industrial regulation.

actors.name: Thai Meteorological Department (TMD)
actors.description: National agency for weather forecasting and climate monitoring.

events.description: Established as Meteorological and Hydrological Service.
Installed Doppler radar system for improved storm tracking.

actors.name: Department of Mineral Resources
actors.description: Manages mineral resources and geological survey.

events.description: Founded as Royal Department of Mines.
Mapped potash deposits in the Northeast.

actors.name: Siam Kubota Corporation
actors.description: Joint venture for tractor and combine harvester manufacturing.

events.description: Established as Siam Kubota Industry Co., Ltd.
Localized production of diesel engines for tractors.

actors.name: Thailand Research Fund (TRF)
actors.description: Primary funding body for basic and applied research.

events.description: Established under the Ministry of Science.
Merged into Thailand Science Research and Innovation (TSRI).

actors.name: National Nanotechnology Center (NANOTEC)
actors.description: Specialized research center for nanoscale technologies.

actors.name: Thai Airways Technics
actors.description: Maintenance, repair, and overhaul center for aviation.

events.description: Established as engineering division of THAI.
Achieved capability to service GE90 engines.

actors.name: Bangkok Industrial Gas (BIG)
actors.description: Supplier of industrial gases critical for manufacturing.

events.description: Built Air Separation Unit in Map Ta Phut.

actors.name: Siam Yamaha Motor
actors.description: Leading motorcycle manufacturer driving engine technology.

events.description: Founded as Siam Yamaha Co., Ltd.
Began local production of engine components.
Merged with Aromatics Thailand to form PTT Global Chemical.

actors.name: Silpakorn University
actors.description: University integrating arts with technology (e.g., architectural tech).

events.description: Founded as the University of Fine Arts.
Established 'Faculty of Information and Communication Technology'.

actors.name: Thailand Convention and Exhibition Bureau (TCEB)
actors.description: Promotes business events and technology trade shows.

actors.name: Thailand Institute of Nuclear Technology (TINT)
actors.description: Research center for nuclear science applications.

events.description: Converted to Airports of Thailand Pcl. (AOT).
Opened Suvarnabhumi Airport with advanced automated baggage systems.

actors.name: Thailand Textile Institute (THTI)
actors.description: Supports textile and garment industry upgrades.

actors.name: Khon Kaen University
actors.description: Leading university in the Northeast, focusing on agriculture and engineering.

events.description: Established the 'Mekong Health Science Research Institute'.

actors.name: Prince of Songkla University (PSU)
actors.description: Leading research university in Southern Thailand.

events.description: Established 'Energy System Research Institute' for rubber biomass.
Initiated 'Smart Grid' pilot project in Pattaya.

actors.name: Metropolitan Electricity Authority (MEA)
actors.description: Distributes electricity to Bangkok and surrounding provinces.

actors.name: Eastern Economic Corridor Office (EECO)
actors.description: Governance body for the Eastern Economic Corridor (EEC).

events.description: Established by the Eastern Special Development Zone Act.
Approved high-speed rail link connecting three airports.

actors.name: Indorama Ventures
actors.description: World's largest producer of PET resin.

actors.name: Chulabhorn Research Institute (CRI)
actors.description: Research institute focusing on chemistry and medicine.

actors.name: Thailand Institute of Directors (IOD)
actors.description: Promotes corporate governance and tech adoption in boards.

actors.name: Thailand Creative & Design Center (TCDC)
actors.description: Promotes design thinking and innovation in industry.

events.description: Established under the Office of Knowledge Management.
Moved to Grand Postal Building, expanding resources.

actors.name: Srinakharinwirot University
actors.description: University focusing on teacher education and science.

events.description: Founded as the Higher Teacher Training School.
Commissioned first HRC (Hot Rolled Coil) plant in Bang Saphan.
Founded as Thai Motor Industry Co., Ltd.
Established production line for Hilux Vigo.
Opened Ban Pho plant, largest Toyota plant in ASEAN.

actors.name: True Digital Park
actors.description: Largest tech and startup hub in Southeast Asia.

events.description: Partnered with Google for startup training.

actors.name: Bitkub
actors.description: Leading cryptocurrency exchange and blockchain developer.

events.description: Received Digital Asset Exchange license from SEC.

actors.name: Flash Group
actors.description: E-commerce logistics provider driven by technology.

events.description: Established as Office of Atomic Energy for Peace.

actors.name: Thai Credit Bank
actors.description: Focus on SME lending and digital banking.

events.description: Converted to Thai Credit Retail Bank.

actors.name: Thailand Office of the Securities and Exchange Commission
Designated Areas for Sustainable Tourism Administration (DASTA)
Techsauce Media
actors.description: Media platform for the Thai startup ecosystem.

actors.name: Thailand Science Research and Innovation (TSRI)
actors.description: Merged entity of TRF and NRCT.

actors.name: Thailand Board of Investment (BOI) - Eastern Economic Corridor Division
actors.description: Specific division targeting high-tech investment in EEC.

actors.name: Bumrungrad International Hospital
actors.description: Pioneer in medical tourism technology adoption.

events.description: Implemented fully integrated hospital information system (HIS).

actors.name: Singha Corporation
actors.description: Beverage giant investing in bio-tech and water tech.

actors.name: Thai Beverage
actors.description: Major F&B player investing in production technology.

actors.name: Pruksa Holding
actors.description: Real estate developer adopting pre-fab technology.

events.description: Adopted Pre-D technology for rapid construction.

actors.name: Sansiri
actors.description: Real estate developer investing in PropTech.

actors.name: PTT Global Chemical (GC)
actors.description: Major player in chemicals and polymers.

actors.name: Global Power Synergy (GPSC)
actors.description: Holding company for PTT's power assets.

actors.name: Banpu
actors.description: Coal miner shifting to renewable energy and tech.

events.description: Partnered with government for 'Digital Thailand' skills.
Formed from CSC and HPE spin-off.

actors.name: Mae Moh Power Plant
actors.description: Lignite-fired power plant, focus on clean coal tech.

actors.name: Map Ta Phut Industrial Estate
actors.description: Hub for petrochemical and heavy industry.

actors.name: Sriracha Tiger Zoo
actors.description: Early adopter of conservation breeding technology.

actors.name: King Mongkut (Rama IV)
actors.description: Father of Thai science and technology; introduced modern astronomy and calculation.

events.description: Calculated and observed solar eclipse in Prachuap Khiri Khan.
Inaugurated the first railway line (Bangkok-Nakhon Ratchasima).

actors.name: King Bhumibol Adulyadej (Rama IX)
actors.description: Held patents for engineering projects; focused on rural development technology.

events.description: Patented the 'Chaipattana Aerator' for water treatment.
First member of royalty to receive WIPO Global Leader Award.

actors.name: Prayut Chan-o-cha
actors.description: PM during launch of Thailand 4.0 policy.

events.description: Launched 'Thailand 4.0' vision at foreign press center.

actors.name: Thaksin Shinawatra
actors.description: Founded Shin Corp (AIS); promoted ICT policies.

actors.name: Dhanin Chearavanont
actors.description: Senior Chairman of CP Group, driving agro-tech and telecom.

events.description: Pioneered entry into telecom sector with TelecomAsia.

actors.name: Kosit Suvinijjit
actors.description: Key figure in establishing NECTEC and early IT policy.

events.description: Appointed Governor of Bank of Thailand, modernizing financial systems.

actors.name: Siam Society
actors.description: Promotes knowledge of science and culture.

actors.name: Thai-Nichi Institute of Technology
actors.description: Vocational education with Japanese industrial standards.

actors.name: Rangsit University
actors.description: Private university with strong medical and engineering faculties.

actors.name: Shinawatra University
actors.description: Private university founded by Thaksin Shinawatra.

actors.name: King Mongkut's University of Technology Thonburi (Ratchaburi Campus)
Chiang Mai Rajabhat University
actors.description: Teacher training to technical education transformation.

actors.name: Office of the Vocational Education Commission (OVEC)

events.description: Founded as Thai Mission School for Mechanics.

actors.name: Krungthai Bank
actors.description: State bank for government tech projects.

actors.name: Mahidol Wittayanusorn School
actors.description: Thailand's first and premier public science school, exclusively for gifted students.

events.description: Established as Princess Sirindhorn's project for gifted science students.
Adopted specialized curriculum developed with IPST (Institute for the Promotion of Teaching Science and Technology).
Opened advanced research labs accessible to high school students.
Students won gold medals at International Olympiad on Astronomy and Astrophysics.

actors.name: Kamnoetvidya Science Academy (KVIS)
actors.description: Top-tier residential science academy founded by PTT Group.

events.description: Founded by PTT Group to create a 'Silicon Valley' pipeline in the East.
Established academic collaboration with foreign universities for student research.
Inaugurated 'Innovation Center' for robotics and AI student projects.

actors.name: Triam Udom Suksa School
actors.description: Most prestigious high school in Thailand, primary feeder for Chulalongkorn University engineering.

events.description: Founded as a preparatory school for Chulalongkorn University.

actors.name: Suankularb Wittayalai School
actors.description: Historic boys' school with strong emphasis on engineering and technical disciplines.

actors.name: Princess Chulabhorn Science High Schools (Network)
actors.description: Network of regional science high schools established to decentralize tech talent development.

actors.name: Samsenwittayalai School
actors.description: High school with a strong technical and vocational stream alongside academic tracks.

events.description: Integrated computer science as a core subject for all levels.

actors.name: Yothinburana School
actors.description: Leading state school with a World Class Standard School designation for science.

events.description: Designated as a 'World Class Standard School' by OBEC.

actors.name: Triam Udom Suksa Nomklao School
actors.description: Sister school to Triam Udom, focusing on science and technology.

events.description: Established to expand science education capacity.

actors.name: Kasetsart University Laboratory School
actors.description: Demonstration school focusing on agriculture and science.

events.description: Piloted hydroponics curriculum for secondary students.

actors.name: Chulalongkorn University Demonstration School
actors.description: Primary feeder for Chula engineering, testing ground for new curricula.

actors.name: Thai-German Technical College
actors.description: Leading vocational college focusing on automotive and mechatronics.

events.description: Certified as a training center for Mercedes-Benz mechanics.
Upgraded to Thai-German Pre-Engineering School status.

actors.name: Thonburi Technical College
actors.description: Major provider of mechanics and electrical technicians.

actors.name: Samutprakarn Technical College
actors.description: Serves the industrial belt of Samut Prakan.

events.description: Dual education program with nearby auto parts manufacturers.

actors.name: Chonburi Technical College
actors.description: Feeder for the Eastern Economic Corridor industries.

events.description: Launched robotics and automation technician course.

actors.name: Rayong Technical College
actors.description: Vital supplier of technicians for petrochemical and auto industries.

actors.name: Nakhon Ratchasima Technical College
actors.description: Largest technical college in the Northeast.

actors.name: Khon Kaen Technical College
actors.description: Key technical institution in the Northeast.

actors.name: Songkhla Technical College
actors.description: Supports the industrial zone in the deep South.

actors.name: Phitsanulok Technical College
actors.description: Technical hub for the Lower North.

actors.name: Udon Thani Technical College
actors.description: Vocational hub for the Upper Northeast.

actors.name: Pattaya Technical College
actors.description: Supports tourism tech and hospitality engineering.

actors.name: Thai-Austrian Technical College
actors.description: Specialized in hospitality and tourism technology.

actors.name: Thai-Japanese Technical College
actors.description: Focuses on electronics and automotive technology.

actors.name: Sri Ayutthaya Technical College
actors.description: Feeder for the electronics cluster in Ayutthaya.

actors.name: Saraburi Technical College
actors.description: Supports heavy industry and ceramics manufacturing.

actors.name: Prachinburi Technical College
actors.description: Feeder for the Eastern Economic Corridor expansion.

actors.name: Chachoengsao Technical College
actors.description: Supports automotive and food processing industries.

actors.name: Rajamangala University of Technology Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya
actors.description: Technical university for heritage and engineering.

Rajamangala University of Technology Srijan (Northern Campus)

actors.name: Ratchamongkol University of Technology Lanna (Tak Campus)
actors.description: Supports logistics and cross-border trade tech.

actors.name: Piboonbumpen Demonstration School (Burapha University)
actors.description: Key science school in the Eastern region.

actors.name: Surin Technical College
actors.description: Technical college for the lower Northeast.

actors.name: Ubon Ratchathani Technical College
actors.description: Technical education for the Mun River basin.

actors.name: Nakhon Si Thammarat Technical College
actors.description: Major technical college in the South.

actors.name: Phuket Technical College
actors.description: Focuses on hospitality technology and marine engineering.

actors.name: Hat Yai Technical College
actors.description: Feeder for the rubber and food processing industries.

actors.name: Ranong Technical College
actors.description: Focus on fisheries and port technology.

actors.name: Narathiwat Technical College
actors.description: Technical education in the deep South.

actors.name: Pattani Technical College
actors.description: Technical college serving the Southern border.

actors.name: Yala Technical College
actors.description: Technical training in the deep South.

actors.name: Trang Technical College
actors.description: Supports rubber and palm oil industries.

actors.name: Krabi Technical College
actors.description: Focus on tourism and palm oil tech.

actors.name: Mukdahan Technical College
actors.description: Mekong cross-border logistics and trade tech.

actors.name: Kalasin Technical College
actors.description: Supports the silk and agricultural industry.

actors.name: Lamphun Technical College
actors.description: Feeder for the Northern Industrial Estate.

actors.name: Lampang Technical College
actors.description: Mining and power generation tech support.

actors.description: Focus on printing and packaging technology.

actors.description: Leading demonstration school for technical pedagogy.

actors.name: Srinakharinwirot University Prasarnmit Demonstration School
actors.description: Highly competitive school for technical talent.

actors.name: Kamnoetvidya Science Academy (Research Center)
actors.description: Research arm of the science academy.

actors.name: Institute for the Promotion of Teaching Science and Technology (IPST)
actors.description: Develops science and math curriculum for schools.
